sections.paragraph:
& קַו with suff. קַוָּם (for קָוֶו, from the root קָוָה, which see)-

(1) a rope, a cord (Arab. قُوَّةُ), 1Ki 7:23 specially-(a) a measuring cord, Eze 47:3. נָטָה קָו עַל to stretch out a measuring cord to measure any thing, Job 38:5 Isa 44:13 especially something to be built, Job loc. cit.; Zec 1:16 to be destroyed, Lam 2:8 2Ki 21:13 comp. Isa 34:11. Hence metaph. rule, law, Isa 28:10. קַו לָקָו קַו לָקָו “(they add) law to law, law to law;” comp. under the word צָו.-(b) the string of a harp; hence sound, Psa 19:5. LXX. φθόγγος. Symm. ἦχος.

(2) strength, might; Arab. قُوَّةُ, see the root No. 2. Isa 18:2, גֹוי קַו־קָו “a very strong nation.” The repetition increases the force.
